title: Listen and Choose a Response — Student Services

questions.stem: I haven't received my financial aid award letter yet. When should I expect it?

options.A: Award letters are being sent out this week. Check your student portal too.
options.B: Financial aid covers tuition and living expenses.
options.C: I applied for a scholarship last month.

questions.explanation: The speaker is asking about a specific timeline for financial aid documents, so providing current information and an alternate way to check is helpful.

questions.stem: I keep forgetting vocabulary terms for the Spanish exam. Any advice?

options.A: Try using flashcards with spaced repetition. That really helped me.
options.B: Spanish exams include grammar and reading comprehension.
options.C: I took Spanish in high school.

questions.explanation: The speaker is asking for study advice, so sharing a specific effective technique is the most helpful response.

questions.stem: My roommate is very noisy and I can't sleep. Can I request a room change?

options.A: I'm sorry to hear that. You can submit a room change request to housing services.
options.C: I get along well with my roommate.

questions.explanation: The speaker is having a housing issue and asking about resolution, so acknowledging their problem and explaining the process is helpful.

questions.stem: You should apply for the teaching assistant position. You'd be perfect for it!

options.A: Thanks for the encouragement, but I'm already committed to the lab assistant role.
options.B: Teaching assistant positions are very competitive.
options.C: I applied for a position last semester.

questions.explanation: The speaker is making a suggestion, so politely declining with gratitude and a valid reason is gracious.

questions.stem: How much does it cost to print in color at the library?

options.A: Color printing is 25 cents per page at the library kiosks.
options.C: I usually print my documents at home.

questions.explanation: The speaker is asking about a specific cost, so providing the exact price is the most direct and helpful response.

questions.stem: I found out I didn't qualify for the study abroad scholarship.

options.A: That's really disappointing. Don't give up — there are other funding options.
options.B: Study abroad scholarships are very limited.
options.C: I'm planning to study abroad next year too.

questions.explanation: The speaker is sharing disappointing news, so expressing empathy and offering hope for alternatives is supportive.

questions.stem: Can I use my student ID to get into the gym, or do I need a separate membership?

options.A: Your student ID works as your gym access card. Just scan it at the entrance.
options.B: The gym has new cardio equipment this semester.
options.C: I go to the gym three times a week.

questions.explanation: The speaker is asking about a specific function of their student ID, so providing a clear answer about usage is most helpful.

questions.stem: I haven't seen you in ages! We should catch up soon.

options.A: Definitely! Are you free this Saturday for coffee?
options.B: I've been really busy with classes.
options.C: Catching up takes too much time.

questions.explanation: The speaker is expressing a desire to reconnect, so enthusiastically agreeing and proposing a specific time is the natural response.

My roommate is very noisy and I can't sleep. Can I request a room change?
